items.title: Intent Engine Using Stale Price and Inaccurate P&L Diagnostics
items.diagnosis: There is a critical data mismatch at Tick 109. The active position legitimately shows the 736P at 0.32 (from 0.22 entry). However, the generated intent state relies on an incorrect contract price of 0.37, derived an inaccurate '+68.2%' P&L, and contradictory reports attribution as 'flat: true'. This indicates the intent model is operating on stale or corrupt price data.
items.proposedAction: Investigate the sync between the position manager and the intent engine. The intent engine is referencing an incorrect contract price (0.37) and incorrect P/L (68.2%) while simultaneously declaring the trade 'flat', all of which contradict the actual position price (0.32) and substantial real gain.
items.experiment: Reproduce on the same replay/build boundary, change one variable only, then compare the finding key and outcome against firstsignal-sim-v1.2-20260712.

items.title: Critical Contradiction: Mindset Engine Guides CALL Execution While Intent Remains Bearish For PUT
items.diagnosis: The system displays radically contradictory intent. The MINDSET engine is actively guiding toward a CALL execution based on bullish narrative, yet the overall Intent state remains PREPARE_PUT with bearish justification, indicating a complete failure of internal logic coherence.
items.proposedAction: Critical review of intent engine logic. Model is guiding toward directional CALL execution (deterministic guide GEX_EXPANSION) while simultaneously projecting standard bearish supporting factors and intent (PREPARE_PUT), creating contradictory signal output. This is a severe logical regression.
items.experiment: Reproduce on the same replay/build boundary, change one variable only, then compare the finding key and outcome against firstsignal-sim-v1.2-20260712.

items.title: Intent Engine Persists with Stale Bearish Factors Despite AI Narrative of Strong Upward Flow
items.diagnosis: The intent engine displays severe incoherence. It justifies a PREPARE_PUT intent based on 'continuing structure' and claims 'Current leg agrees,' while the AI journal prose simultaneously details active upward flow, re-expanding positive leads, and targeted higher prices, meaning the immediate leg does not agree with a PUT thesis.
items.proposedAction: Investigate intent engine supporting factor mapping. Supporting factors like 'Current leg agrees' must reflect the immediate price action described in the AI narrative, rather than persisting with stale bearish descriptors during active counter-trend flow.
items.experiment: Reproduce on the same replay/build boundary, change one variable only, then compare the finding key and outcome against firstsignal-sim-v1.2-20260712.

items.title: Intent Engine Ignores Thesis Invalidation Caused by Positive GEX Swap
items.diagnosis: The Intent Engine persists with PREPARE_PUT, citing "continuing structure" and claiming the "Current leg agrees," despite immediate journal evidence at 12:55 PM explicitly stating that the downside thesis is fully invalidated because the GEX swapped to positive. This indicates a profound disconnect between dynamic structural data and intent generation.
items.proposedAction: Correct the intent engine's logic to acknowledge definitive structural shifts, such as GEX flipping positive, to prevent the maintenance of a fundamentally invalidated short-side bias.
items.experiment: Reproduce on the same replay/build boundary, change one variable only, then compare the finding key and outcome against firstsignal-sim-v1.2-20260712.

items.title: Intent Engine Claims Leg Agreement Despite AI Recognition of Sluggish Stasis and Timing Failure

It justifies a PREPARE_PUT action claiming "Current leg agrees," while the simultaneous AI journal entry at 2:17 PM reports that the "Violent trigger through 745.0 is still missing" and that the tactical timing has failed due to sluggishness. The current leg explicitly does not agree with a valid entry thesis.
items.proposedAction: Force the intent engine to acknowledge stasis and lack of immediate confirmation, aligning its "Current leg agrees" assessment with the AI journal's definition of timing failure.
items.experiment: Reproduce on the same replay/build boundary, change one variable only, then compare the finding key and outcome against firstsignal-sim-v1.2-20260712.
